It’s the eye of the lizard, it’s the thrill of the fight…

10 Oct 2017

At Pua’a Ka’a State Park outside Hana, Hawai’i.

10 Oct 2017

Word to the wise…

09 Oct 2017

The Road to Hana is paved with good banana bread.

09 Oct 2017

Outside the Ritz-Carlton Kapalua. The lizard was staying there, but we weren’t.

08 Oct 2017

Discovered a new item for my bucket list at the same time I checked it off today:

☑️ Swim with sea turtles

07 Oct 2017

Lying on Kapalua Bay Beach, listening to the waves, and watching the clouds build on Molokai.

06 Oct 2017

Wheels for the week. Trying on the Jeep life.

06 Oct 2017

On board. 🛫🛬🌴

05 Oct 2017

OH: If the gummi bears are made of the wrong stuff you don’t need to use a chain saw.

03 Oct 2017

Accidental nine run nap. Go Cubs Go!

30 Sep 2017

“Their specialty is the communication of rage to an audience prone to histrionics. It can feel awfully good to be awfully mad.” —Bret Stephens

30 Sep 2017

It’s a beautiful morning to celebrate another NL Central championship. Go Cubs Go!

28 Sep 2017

And then company engineering chat becomes a discussion of how to implement NSDate comparisons in relativistic reference frames. 🚀

26 Sep 2017

Marina from Sunset Hill at dawn. There are some benefits to not being able to sleep.

26 Sep 2017

“The bottom line is that the bill for cynicism seems to be coming due. For years, flat-out lies about policy served Republicans well… But those same lies now leave them unable to govern.” —Paul Krugman

25 Sep 2017

“America is now two countries, eyeing each other across a chasm of distrust and contempt. One is urban, diverse and outward-looking. This is the America that’s growing. The other is white, provincial and culturally revanchist. This is the America that’s in charge.” —Michelle Goldberg

25 Sep 2017

On top of Poe Mountain in the Glacier Peak Wilderness.

24 Sep 2017

“But what if rigid support for single-payer…becomes a litmus test? … Democrats could eventually find themselves facing a Trumpcare-type debacle, unable either to implement their unrealistic vision or to let it go.” —Paul Krugman

15 Sep 2017

I was better off before I knew that this existed.
